summary: Drew DeVault announces he is handing over maintenance of wlroots and sway to Simon Ser. He explains that he has accomplished everything he set out to do with Wayland and has been gradually reducing his involvement. Simon has been a key co-maintainer for years and also maintains Wayland and Weston. DeVault notes that Simon works full-time at SourceHut, ensuring continued project health.
key_points: DeVault considers his Wayland goals fully accomplished, with sway serving as a reliable daily driver
Simon Ser has been co-maintaining wlroots and sway for years, making this a smooth transition rather than an abrupt handoff
SourceHut compensates Simon's work on Wayland projects, ensuring sustainable long-term maintenance
The projects have already been functioning well without DeVault's active involvement
money_quotes: I feel that I've already accomplished everything I set out to do with Wayland — and more!
I have been happily using sway as my daily driver for well over a year with no complaints or conspicuously absent features.
For me, there's little reason to stay involved.
Simon works for SourceHut full-time and his compensation covers his role in the Wayland community, so you can trust that the health of the project is unaffected, too.
sway and wlroots have already demonstrated that they work quite well without my active involvement.
key_insight: A healthy open source project is one that can thrive beyond its original creator, and a good maintainer knows when to step back and let capable successors lead.
